root_cause: Calibrated prior fusion v2 (run-0002-0002) scored 0.6648 val ROC-AUC, a -0.0005 regression vs the leader run-0003-0001 (0.6652). The prior fusion component is near-random on train (0.487) and below raw probe on validation (0.662), confirming Bayesian prior calibration extracts no meaningful signal. The prior fusion branch is exhausted after two failed attempts (run-0003-0002 and run-0002-0002).
why: The prior fusion post-processing lane has been tested twice with consistent negative results. Both negative vetoes at confidence 0.68 confirm this axis should be closed. The immediate priority is shipping the critic-approved raw-probe leader bundle (run-0003-0001 at 0.6652) to secure a submission slot. After that, plan should target high-value axes: probe-head architecture improvements (MLP regularization without prior fusion) and revised class-coverage strategies, since brute-force 52→75 expansion was flat. Collapsing to another calibration tweak would waste experiment capacity with no signal basis.
next_title: Probe-head architecture and revised coverage strategy
portfolio_intent: Close prior fusion (tertiary, exhausted). Ship primary data_coverage leader. Open probe-head architecture as next experimental axis.

problem_frame.focus: Ship the raw-probe leader submission bundle and open a probe-head architecture experiment axis. Prior fusion is exhausted.
problem_frame.root_cause: Prior fusion has been tested twice with negative results; the Bayesian prior adds no signal. The leader at 0.6652 is critic-approved and ready for submission. Next gains likely come from probe-head architecture rather than post-processing.
